region_of_origin: North America (Pacific Coast); commonly served in Singapore Chinese cuisine

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 227261009 covers crab as a food substance broadly; no Dungeness-specific SNOMED code identified.

serving.description: 1 serving (approx. half a crab, edible meat)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Dungeness crab in a Singapore Chinese restaurant is approximately 150 g of edible crab meat, equivalent to roughly half a medium-sized crab.

nutrient_source.title: Crustaceans, crab, dungeness, cooked, moist heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174202)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced from Singapore HPB Foodo canonical record G00010771 for Dungeness Crab, which is an approved primary regional source. Supplementary micronutrients not reported by HPB (saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, vitamins, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, water) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174202 for Dungeness crab cooked moist heat, which is an exact species and preparation match and an approved secondary global source. Both HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy are on the approved source list per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Dungeness crab contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g (confirmed per HPB Foodo G00010771 and USDA FDC 174202). Glycemic index is not applicable for foods with negligible carbohydrate content per the University of Sydney GI Database methodology. The GI Database does not assign GI values to pure protein or fat foods as they produce no measurable glycaemic response. Glycemic index is set to null; gi_category is set to not_applicable; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 395 mg per 100 g — borderline high for a single seafood serving; potassium 259 mg provides partial natriuretic offset
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit Dungeness crab to a single 150 g serving and strictly avoid adding high-sodium condiments such as soy sauce or oyster sauce, which can raise the total sodium load of the meal above 800 mg; request steamed or boiled preparation without added salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein 17.88 g per 100 g slows gastric emptying and has no direct glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dungeness crab is an excellent protein source for people with Type 2 Diabetes due to its zero carbohydrate content and negligible fat; consume steamed or boiled without sweet or starchy dipping sauces to preserve the glycaemic benefit, and monitor total dietary cholesterol given 97 mg per 100 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat 0.74 g per 100 g with favourable polyunsaturated fatty acid profile including EPA/DHA omega-3; cholesterol 97 mg per 100 g is moderate
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Dungeness crab is cardioprotective due to its omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA), very low saturated fat (~0.1 g per 100 g), and high lean protein; patients with cardiovascular disease should consume it steamed or boiled without added fats, limit portion to 150 g to manage sodium intake, and avoid high-sodium or high-fat sauces.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dungeness crab (Cancer magister) is a large Pacific coast crustacean widely consumed in Singapore Chinese cuisine, typically served steamed or boiled whole and presented at the table for communal eating. The flesh is sweet, delicate, and slightly briny with a firm yet tender texture. It is prized for its high lean protein content (17.88 g per 100 g), negligible fat (0.74 g), and zero carbohydrate, making it one of the most nutritionally efficient seafood choices available. The crab is naturally rich in vitamin B12 (9.78 mcg per 100 g, over 400% of daily reference intake), selenium (40.5 mcg), zinc (4.03 mg), phosphorus (229 mg), and calcium (91 mg), supporting neurological function, immune health, and bone integrity. Sodium is naturally elevated at 395 mg per 100 g due to the marine environment, which requires attention for hypertensive diners. Omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) are present in the polyunsaturated fat fraction and contribute anti-inflammatory cardiovascular benefit. In Singapore, Dungeness crab is commonly prepared as steamed crab with ginger and scallion, chilli crab (with added sauce), or black pepper crab, though the nutritional profile here reflects the plain cooked crab meat without sauce additions. Clinically, it is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management (zero glycaemic impact), conditionally suitable for hypertension (sodium awareness required), and beneficial for cardiovascular health due to its omega-3 and lean protein profile.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from Singapore HPB Foodo canonical record G00010771 (approved primary regional source, confidence 0.95). Supplementary micronutrients sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174202 for Dungeness crab cooked moist heat, which is an exact species and preparation match and an approved secondary global source. Both sources are explicitly on the approved list per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as Dungeness crab is a pure animal protein with no carbohydrate-containing tissue; confirmed zero per Singapore HPB Foodo G00010771 and USDA FDC 174202. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal tissue contains no dietary fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed trace/negligible per USDA FDC 174202). Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB G00010771; USDA FDC 174202 does not report retinol activity equivalents for this entry. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB G00010771; USDA FDC 174202 does not report vitamin D for this entry. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB G00010771; USDA FDC 174202 does not report vitamin E for this entry. Halal status is marked false as Dungeness crab (crustacean) is not universally accepted as halal under all Islamic jurisprudence schools (Hanafi school considers crustaceans non-halal); consumers should verify with their own religious authority. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g carbohydrate per 100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_index set to null per the critical rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign GI values to pure protein foods as they produce no measurable glycaemic response. The health_suitability entry for rheumatoid_arthritis in the input JSON contained an internal contradiction (the reason stated anti-inflammatory benefits but suitable was set to false); this has been excluded from health_context as rheumatoid_arthritis is not a mandatory condition and the input data was internally inconsistent. The three mandatory health conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) are fully populated with clinical specificity.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Dungeness Crab', a precise canonical English species name. All five mandatory Singaporean-cuisine languages are present: English (food_name), Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal 83.0, protein_g 17.88, fat_g 0.74, carbohydrate_g 0.0 all present and numeric. Carbohydrate zero is justified in notes as naturally zero confirmed by HPB G00010771 and USDA FDC 174202. nutrient_source cites USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174202 as the primary approved source title, with HPB Foodo G00010771 documented in source_match_notes as the canonical Singapore primary source for core macros. Both are approved sources per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md. source_match_type is 'adapted' reflecting the combination of HPB primary and USDA supplementary data.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.0 (naturally zero, animal tissue), sodium_mg 395.0, saturated_fat_g 0.1, sugar_g 0.0 (naturally zero) — all present as numeric values, plausible and supported by HPB G00010771 and USDA FDC 174202.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to null (not 0 — correct per critical rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods), gi_category set to not_applicable, glycemic_load_per_serving 0.0, gi_source organization set to University of Sydney GI Database with explanation that the database does not assign GI values to pure protein foods. source_match_type is analogous_estimate with full documentation of the not-applicable rationale. This satisfies Q4 for foods with negligible carbohydrates.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for Dungeness crab whole/parts. FoodEx2 code A0CJW for Crab Dungeness is a defensible exact match. SNOMED CT 227261009 used as closest_parent match with gap note. No codes invented.

Covers species identity, preparation methods, taste and texture, cultural context in Singapore, clinical relevance for diabetes/hypertension/CVD, and key nutrient highlights.

q7_health_context.notes: All three mandatory conditions present: Hypertension (moderate concern, sodium-focused guidance), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, zero-carb guidance with cholesterol caveat), Cardiovascular Disease (low concern, omega-3 and lean protein guidance). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly.

G2 approved-source compliance is now satisfied: nutrient_source explicitly references USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174202 (approved secondary global source) as the titled entry with HPB Foodo G00010771 (approved primary regional source) documented in source_match_notes; gi_source.organization is now explicitly 'University of Sydney GI Database' with methodology explanation for negligible-carbohydrate foods. Q4 glycemic completeness is satisfied with glycemic_index as null (not 0), gi_category as not_applicable, glycemic_load_per_serving as 0.0, and gi_source referencing the University of Sydney GI Database. Three mandatory health conditions are fully populated with clinical specificity. Ontology anchors are defensible and not invented. LLM description exceeds 200 characters.
